scope.topic: Taobao search feedback and AI shopping-guide opportunities
scope.sampling_note: Xiaohongshu CLI and browser scraping attempts were blocked by captcha / missing extension access during this run. The report therefore treats Xiaohongshu findings as directional social-listening signals, triangulated with public indexed discussion, industry reports, Alibaba product news, and search-intent literature rather than exhaustive platform data.

negative.summary: Users complain that exact product terms produce results shaped by recent viewing, ads, shops, or generic recommendation logic rather than the typed query.
negative.implication: Separate task search from feed-style discovery; expose why an item was included.

negative.summary: Clothing searches are often described as visually repetitive, with many near-identical SKUs and unclear differences in material, version, fit, or source.
negative.implication: AI should cluster duplicates, surface decision variables, and explain trade-offs instead of merely listing more items.

negative.summary: Users struggle to rediscover an item from a partial memory, screenshot, influencer note, previous order, or vague occasion requirement.
negative.implication: AI search should accept fuzzy multi-modal input and bridge search, order history, and image recognition.

negative.summary: Consumers repeatedly ask whether a visible price is the final hand price, whether coupons stack, and whether similar items are cheaper elsewhere in Taobao.
negative.implication: The AI guide must calculate effective price, coupon eligibility, logistics, and risk before recommendation.

positive.theme: Image search remains a strong habit
positive.summary: Users still rely on Taobao when they have screenshots, product pictures, or style references.
positive.implication: AI guide should start from image search strength and add semantic understanding on top.

positive.summary: Taobao is still perceived as the place with broad SKU coverage and niche alternatives.
positive.implication: AI search can turn supply breadth from cognitive load into advantage by filtering and comparing.

positive.summary: Users expect Taobao to help with coupons, substitutes, store comparison, and timing around campaigns.
positive.implication: AI guide can become a transactional assistant, not only a Q&A surface.

queryDistribution.label: Exact product / known item
queryDistribution.why: Needs precise recall, duplicate merging, image-text alignment, and trust.

queryDistribution.label: Category browse / broad need
queryDistribution.why: Needs faceted narrowing and preference elicitation before ranking.

queryDistribution.label: Attribute / constraint search
queryDistribution.why: Natural-language constraints are hard to express with traditional filters.

queryDistribution.label: Occasion / solution search
queryDistribution.why: The user is buying an outcome, not a SKU; AI can decompose the job.

queryDistribution.label: Comparison / decision
queryDistribution.why: Requires synthesis of price, reviews, store trust, and scenario fit.

queryDistribution.label: Order / after-sale / local service
queryDistribution.why: Search should connect with personal transaction context.

queryDistribution.label: Content-inspired shopping
queryDistribution.why: Needs cross-platform semantic grounding and visual matching.

scenarioMap.userNeed: I remember what it looked like or where I saw it, but not the exact product title.
scenarioMap.aiMove: Combine image search, title memory, order history, browsing trail, and style embeddings to reconstruct candidate sets.
scenarioMap.surface: AI search tab + search box suggestion

scenarioMap.userNeed: I need a style that fits my body, occasion, budget, and material preferences.
scenarioMap.aiMove: Ask 1-2 clarifying questions, extract constraints, cluster similar SKUs, and explain differences in fit/material/version.

scenarioMap.userNeed: Tell me the final hand price and whether this is actually worth buying now.
scenarioMap.aiMove: Calculate coupon stacking, campaign timing, shipping, return risk, historical price, and substitutes.
scenarioMap.surface: Product list badge + AI guide panel

scenarioMap.userNeed: I am solving a job: moving home, camping, new baby, first office setup.
scenarioMap.aiMove: Convert the job into a checklist, recommend bundles, identify missing items, and allow user to remove/replace.

scenarioMap.userNeed: I need to know what can go wrong, not just the seller's selling points.
scenarioMap.aiMove: Summarize reviews by scenario, flag size/material/service risks, and show evidence snippets.

sources.name: Alibaba / media coverage on Taobao AI Search and AI shopping assistance
sources.use: Validate that Taobao is already moving from search-box interaction toward AI guide surfaces and AI search capabilities.

sources.name: Baymard Institute e-commerce search query type research
sources.use: Ground the query taxonomy in e-commerce-specific query classes such as exact, product type, feature, thematic, compatibility, symptom, non-product and subjective queries.

sources.name: Broder and Jansen search-intent literature
sources.use: Frame search intent as navigational, informational and transactional; adapt to Taobao's transaction-heavy context.

sources.name: Alibaba product search / LLM retrieval research including recent Taobao AI Search papers
sources.use: Support technical feasibility of semantic retrieval, learning-to-rank, and LLM-based reranking in Taobao-scale product search.
